title: Bao-Dur
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Citation}} {{Character |type=Jedi |image=[[File:BaoDur_Rener.png]] |name=Bao-Dur |homeworld=[[Iridonia/Legends|Iridonia]] |birth= |death= |species=[[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]]''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Black]] {{C|eyebrows}} |feathers= |eyes=Gold |skin=Grayish brown |cyber=[[Energy-arm]] |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] *[[Lost Jedi]] *[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] |masters=[[Meetra Surik]] {{C|Informal [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]}} |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Having you here has an effect on me, General. I never noticed it years ago. I think my mind was too occupied then.|Bao-Dur, to Meetra Surik|''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''}} '''Bao-Dur''' {{Pronounced|beo dur}} was a [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and [[Engineer/Legends|engineer]] from [[Iridonia/Legends|Iridonia]]. He served under the [[Revanchist]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] general [[Meetra Surik]] during the [[Mandalorian Wars]], and again toward the end of the [[Dark Wars]]. He was a skilled inventor and was responsible for the design, construction and activation of the [[Mass Shadow Generator/Legends|Mass Shadow Generator]]. Years after the war he helped Surik in stopping the echo in [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] caused by the Mass Shadow Generator and defeating the [[Sith Triumvirate]]. Wherever he went he was always accompanied by a small, spherical [[Bao-Dur's remote|remote]] which he had built as a child. ==Biography== ===Mandalorian Wars=== ====War's start==== When the [[Mandalorian Wars]] began, many [[Iridonia/Legends|Iridonian]] colonies were attacked and destroyed first. When Bao-Dur heard of the attacks, he leapt at the opportunity to join the war when the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] began to fight back. However, it was not because he wanted to stop the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorians]], but because he wished to get revenge on the beings who had slaughtered so many of his people. He was the only Iridonian [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]] who served in his unit. Bao-Dur specialized as a [[Technician/Legends|technician]] during the war, eventually coming to serve under the [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] [[Meetra Surik]]. He was the inventor of many new weapons, shields, and other accessories. His most infamous weapon was the [[Mass Shadow Generator/Legends|Mass Shadow Generator]], which he designed and built after getting a Republic military commission as a [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]]. ====Malachor V==== During the [[Battle of Malachor V]], Surik, who later befriended Bao-Dur, gave the command to activate the weapon with a wordless nod, thereby [[Death/Legends|killing]] almost everyone fighting in the battle above [[Malachor V]], Mandalorian and Republic alike. The devastation created, as the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] perceived it, a massive [[Wound in the Force/Legends|wound in the Force]] which cast echoes throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. During the battle, Bao-Dur lost his arm, which he replaced with a [[Energy-arm|new mechanical arm]]. ===Later years=== During the [[Jedi Civil War]] Bao-Dur's [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] colony in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] was bombed out of commission by [[Darth Malak]].[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords'': Prima Official Game Guide]] After the war's end, Bao-Dur decided to use his mechanical genius to help people rather than design weapons. Originally he intended to design planetary shield generators, but most governments were unable to afford them. Instead, he joined the Republic-sponsored [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorian]] herd that was aiding the [[Telosian Restoration Project|Restoration Project]] on [[Telos IV]], a world whose surface had been obliterated by a [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith]] fleet during the [[Jedi Civil War]]. He was in charge of the shield system that cordoned off areas on the Telosian surface and provided protection for any animals introduced there. He worked there so that he might help to add back into the galaxy some of the life he took away at Malachor V. [[Atris]] and her [[Handmaiden Sisters|Handmaidens]] generally thought highly of Bao-Dur's skill with machinery and technology, with [[Brianna]] saying that his skills with shields were greater than some [[Echani/Legends|Echani]] shieldsmiths. During his time on Telos, Bao-Dur came to hate the [[Czerka Arms/Legends|Czerka Corporation]], and when the Telosian government was ineffective at stopping their depredations, he traveled to the planet's surface to stop them himself. However, the numerous [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] garrisoned by Czerka on Telos' surface forced him into hiding. Shortly afterward, a shuttle crashed not far from his hiding place. ===Travels with Surik=== {{Quote|Can you build me one?''"
"''I would, but then I wouldn't be the only one with a floating sphere following him around, and I kind of like that.|Meetra Surik and Bao-Dur, about [[Bao-Dur's remote]]|}} When Bao-Dur went to [[Skirmish on Telos|investigate]] he found a burning wreckage and went in to save the crew of three. Among them was Meetra Surik, now branded the Jedi Exile. Although she did not recognize him at first, Bao-Dur still insisted on referring to her as "General." Bao-Dur traveled with Surik throughout the galaxy, helping her at [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]], [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]], and [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]]. During the travels, she found that he was [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]], so he agreed to let Surik train him in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] as a [[Jedi Guardian/Legends|Jedi Guardian]]. [[File:Remotefinalmission.jpg|thumb|left|Bao-Dur sends his remote on one final mission.|202x202px]] Eventually, [[Kreia]] betrayed the group and revealed her true identity as Darth Traya. After the [[Battle of Telos IV]], Surik and her group went to Malachor V to track Traya. On the descent, the ''[[Ebon Hawk]]'' crashed, and the entire crew save Surik and [[Mira]] were incapacitated. However, Bao-Dur had previously programed his [[Remote/Legends|remote]] to reactivate the Mass Shadow Generator for a final time if Surik should give the order. The remote managed to follow through, and though it activated the generator, it was destroyed along with Malachor. Traya could not read either his future or those of the droids [[T3-M4]], [[HK-47]], and [[G0-T0]]. However, Traya did reveal that the Force-sensitive crew of the ''Hawk'' had been the [[Lost Jedi]]; therefore it is likely that Bao-Dur had a part in rebuilding the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], while Surik left known space to search for [[Revan/Legends|Revan]]. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|I'll take the stupid one, who decided to threaten us rather than shoot us when he had the chance.|Bao-Dur, after being confronted by the [[Zhug brothers]]|}} Although he joined the Republic military for the sake of revenge, Bao-Dur had a deeper conscience after the events of Malachor V. Wracked with guilt over creating the Mass Shadow Generator, he wandered into exile, hopping from planet to planet, before he went to Telos IV and dedicated his life to helping others. The war took an obvious emotional toll on him. Although he was generally amiable, he held a deep prejudice against Mandalorians for their actions in the wars and frequently clashed with [[Canderous Ordo]] over matters concerning honor. He was, for the most part, a mild-mannered person of few words with an appreciation for the small things in life, and a blithely sarcastic sense of humor. Although he trusted Surik and felt as though she was one of the few people who could understand the effect that the Mandalorian Wars had on those who had fought in them, he did not often speak except on matters at hand. He enjoyed the natural beauty of the restored areas of Telos and liked to see acts of kindness. However, he hated seeing the malicious or greedy harming others and did not hesitate in stepping forward to stop them, even to the extent of trying to stand alone against Czerka. Aside from [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]] he could speak [[Zabraki/Legends|Zabraki]], [[Bocce/Legends|Bocce]], [[Ithorese/Legends|Ithorese]] and [[Binary/Legends|Binary]] languages. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Baodurconcept.png|thumb|120px|Bao-Dur concept art]] {{Quote|How'd you lose your arm anyway?''"
"''I got tired of it - kept dropping my hydrospanner. Figured I'd get a new one.''"
"''Yeah, I'll bet that was fun.|Meetra Surik and Bao-Dur, talking about his arm|}} Bao-Dur was created by [[Obsidian Entertainment]] as one of the companions for the [[2004]] [[Wikipedia:Xbox|Xbox]] and [[Wikipedia:Personal computer|PC]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''. He was written by [[Michael Chu]].{{WebCite|url=http://www.cinelinx.com/news/culture/knights-of-the-old-republic-ii-developer-chris-avellone-discusses-the-games-impact-15-years-later|text=Knights of the Old Republic II Developer Chris Avellone Discusses the Game's Impact 15 Years Later|work=[https://www.cinelinx.com Cinelinx]|archivedate=20191208220416|author=Maison, Jordan|date=2019-12-06|quote=I had to write almost all the companions, except for Mandalore and Bao-Dur (who was written by Michael Chu[)]}} He is voiced by [[Roger Guenveur Smith|Roger G. Smith]]. While referred to as "Bao-Dur" throughout most of the game, he is credited as "Bao Dur," without the hyphen. This form also appears at some points in the game, mainly in rushed conversations like his hologram's final order to his remote. His concept art was realized by [[Brian Menze]], based on a description by [[Dave Maldonado]]. Maldonado was the one who first described Bao-Dur's mechanical arm.{{DeviantArt|username=brenze|url=comments/1/147112880/2809155254|name=[[Brian Menze]]|text=Comment on Bao Dur by Brenze}} Bao-Dur's character class, Tech Specialist, did not appear in the first ''KotOR'' game and is unique to him in the sequel, although it does exist in the [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]. If Surik chooses to train him in the ways of the Force, he becomes a Jedi Guardian. If he is seduced by the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] due to Surik's influence, he begins to display [[Sith tattoo]]s similar to those of [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]], who shares his species; it is difficult for the player, however, to gain influence with Bao-Dur while dark-side without taking an "alignment hit" since he respects light-side acts and ''despises'' dark-side acts. [[File:Baotattoo.jpg|thumb|left|150x|Bao-Dur, immersed in the dark side.]] In the cut material from the game's ending, no mention of Bao-Dur was made at all. Despite the fact that Canderous Ordo can be seen with Visas on the Malachor surface loading screen, some fans speculate that Bao-Dur and Canderous Ordo did not join Surik on the trip to Malachor V since they were not to appear to attack Kreia at the end of the game. It is rumored that he was to be killed, since he only appears as a hologram on Malachor, and his place in the party selection screen is filled with an image of his remote. It is also possible that Kreia's inability to predict his fate was meant to leave room for his character to reappear along with the droids in a possible third installment in the series. In an interview, [[Chris Avellone]] stated that Bao-Dur was intended to die during the [[Battle of Telos IV]], while helping HK-47 reach and shut down the [[HK Manufacturing Plant]].{{WebCite|url=https://forums.obsidian.net/blogs/entry/120-kotor-ii-questions|text=Kotor II Questions|author=[[Chris Avellone]]|date=2009-10-15|work=[[Obsidian Entertainment]]'s official forums|archivedate=20200612181330}} Additionally, in cut content, Bao-Dur reveals that he and the [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]] mechanic, [[Tien Tubb]], worked together in a hangar bay during the Mandalorian Wars. This also reveals that Bao-Dur was part of the [[12th Engineering Division]] out of Iridonia. ==Appearances== *''[[The Old Republic: Revan]]'' {{Ab|The Old Republic: Revan (audiobook)}} {{Imo}} *''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' {{1st}} *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Rise of the Hutt Cartel]]'' {{Mo}} ==Sources== [[File:Bao-Dur_KOTORCG.jpg|thumb|200px|Bao-Dur]] {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Bao-Dur}} *[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords'': Prima Official Game Guide]] *''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide]]'' *''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' *{{SWMiniCite|set=Knights of the Old Republic|pack=Bao-Dur}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Clone Wars Campaign Guide]]'' *''[[Jedi Academy Training Manual]]'' *''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide: Updated and Expanded]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *{{DeviantArt|username=brenze|url=brenze/art/Bao-Dur-147112880|name=[[Brian Menze]]|text=Bao Dur}} {{K2char}} {{Interlang |de=Bao-Dur#legends |es=Bao-Dur |fr=Bao-Dur |hu=Bao-Dur |pl=Bao-Dur |pt=Legends:Bao-Dur |deFA=1 }} [[Category:Cyborgs with prosthetics]] [[Category:Engineers]] [[Category:Galactic Republic military personnel]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Individuals with lost limbs]] [[Category:Jedi Guardians]] [[Category:Jedi trainees of the Jedi Order]] [[Category:Lost Jedi (Meetra Surik)]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Mechanics]] [[Category:Slicers]] [[Category:Zabrak]]
